creator: tech@integritydata.com.au
text:
Have installed a 'basic' copy of 4.2 on a Gateway 5300 Laptop. dmesg reports the pccard items as follows;

pcic0:  <Intel i82365> at port 0x3e0 iomem 0xd0000 on isa0
pcic0:  Polling mode

The following PC Cards have all been tried;

Lucent Technologies WaveLAN IEEE
3Com EtherLink III 3C589C
NE2000 PCMCIA Ethernet Card

All three of these cards work in other laptops configured with FreeBSD 4.2

When starting pccardd with the -d option the following is displayed;

# pccardd -d
Code 85 not found
Code 85 not found
code Unknown ignored
Code 47 not found
Code 47 not found
code Unknown ignored
Code 10 not found
Code 10 not found
code Unknown ignored
Code 114 not found
Code 114 not found
code Unknown ignored
Mar 30 11:04:51 pccardd[203]: No card in database for "(null)"("(null)")
Mar 30 11:04:51 pccardd[203]: No card in database for "(null)"("(null)")
Mar 30 11:04:51 pccardd[203]: No card in database for "(null)"("(null)")
Mar 30 11:04:51 pccardd[203]: pccardd started
Mar 30 11:04:51 pccardd[203]: pccardd started
Mar 30 11:04:51 pccardd[203]: pccardd started

Files in etc/defaults are stock standard and have not been changed!
/etc/defaults/rc.conf has pc card support enabled.

How-To-Repeat: Get a Gateway 5300 laptop, install FreeBSD 4.2 and enable pccard support, then insert any pccard.
